troubleshooting_database.error: No matching function for fit(vector, float, float)
troubleshooting_database.fix: Check the signature of 'fit'. You are likely passing the wrong number of arguments or incorrect types (e.g., float instead of vector).
The function 'fit' does not exist or is not available in the current context (e.g., a SOP function used in a CVEX context).
VEX cannot decide which overload of 'fit' to use. Cast your arguments explicitly, e.g., float(val).
The attribute 'myAttr' does not exist on the geometry. Ensure it is created before this wrangle or use a default value: @myAttr = 0;
Check for missing semicolons, unmatched parentheses, or typos around the word 'if'.
Add a small epsilon value to the divisor to prevent mathematical errors: 1.0/x / (val + 1e-6).
The array only has 5 elements. Use the len() function to check bounds before accessing index 6.
The attribute 'myAttr' was previously defined as a different type (e.g., float vs vector). Use a different name or force the type with a prefix like v@myAttr.

troubleshooting_database.error: hou.ObjectWasDeleted: Attempt to access a deleted Node
troubleshooting_database.fix: The Node reference is stale. Re-fetch the object using hou.node() or hou.item() before calling methods on it.
The preceding hou.node() or hou.parm() call returned None. Verify that the path string is correct and the node exists.
The node is likely inside a locked HDA or is a managed node. Allow editing on the HDA instance or unlock the node.

troubleshooting_database.error: TypeError: createNode() takes exactly 2 arguments (1 given)
troubleshooting_database.fix: Check the Houdini Python documentation for 'createNode'. You are passing an incorrect number of parameters.

troubleshooting_database.error: hou.OperationFailed: The attempted operation failed on /obj/geo1
troubleshooting_database.fix: Check the Houdini console for more details. This often happens when creating a node of an invalid type or in the wrong context.

troubleshooting_database.error: IndexError: list index out of range
troubleshooting_database.fix: You are trying to access an element in a list (like points or nodes) that doesn't exist. Check the length of the list first.
Verify the file path exists and is accessible. Use hou.expandString('$HIP') to debug path variables.

troubleshooting_database.fix: Check the Houdini console for more details. This often happens when creating a node of an invalid type or in the wrong context.

troubleshooting_database.error: IndexError: list index out of range
troubleshooting_database.fix: You are trying to access an element in a list (like points or nodes) that doesn't exist. Check the length of the list first.
Verify the file path exists and is accessible. Use hou.expandString('$HIP') to debug path variables.

troubleshooting_database.error: hou.ObjectWasDeleted: Attempt to access a deleted Node
troubleshooting_database.fix: The Node reference is stale. Re-fetch the object using hou.node() or hou.item() before calling methods on it.
The preceding hou.node() or hou.parm() call returned None. Verify that the path string is correct and the node exists.

troubleshooting_database.error: Invalid source at input 0 of polyextrude1
troubleshooting_database.fix: The node connected to input 0 is not providing the correct data type or is errored. Check the upstream network.
A node upstream of this input has an error. Follow the red error flags up the chain to find the root cause.
A parameter or expression in 'polyextrude1' is referencing itself or a child node, creating a circular logic loop.

troubleshooting_database.error: Unable to read geometry file: model.obj
troubleshooting_database.fix: The file path is incorrect or the format is unsupported. Ensure the file extension matches the data (e.g., .bgeo.sc, .obj, .abc).
The node 'polyextrude1' expects an attribute named 'N' which was not found. Add an Attribute Create or Wrangle node upstream.
This node requires a connection on input 0 to function. Connect a valid operator.
The input geometry is empty or missing expected components like primitives (points, primitives, or vertices).

troubleshooting_database.error: Object 'collider_geo' has no geometry for collision
troubleshooting_database.fix: The static or rigid body object is referencing an empty SOP path. Assign a valid geometry node.
The simulation is unstable. Try increasing the 'Min Substeps' or decreasing the 'Time Step' on the DOP Network.
The solver expects data type 'Velocity' (e.g., Position, Velocity) which is missing from the input stream.
The collision representation (Volume, Bullet, SDF) is not supported by the current solver. Change the 'Collision Representation' parameter.

troubleshooting_database.error: FLIP fluid is leaking from collider_geo
troubleshooting_database.fix: The collision geometry is too thin or has holes. Increase the collision thickness or the grid resolution of the FLIP solver.
Bullet solver works best with convex hulls. Switch the collision type to 'Convex Hull' or decompose the geometry into convex pieces.
Check the simulation start frame and ensure all required objects (Object, Geometry, Data) are connected to the merge node.

Ensure the render engine (Karma, Mantra, Redshift) is installed and the license is active.

troubleshooting_database.error: Geometry attribute 'Cd' not exported to renderer
troubleshooting_database.fix: Check the ROP node settings to ensure the attribute 'Cd' is included in the 'Attributes to Export' list.

troubleshooting_database.error: Sampling error in material /mat/principled1: Out of memory
troubleshooting_database.fix: The shader is too complex or the texture resolution is too high for the available GPU/CPU memory. Optimize your textures or simplify the shader graph.

troubleshooting_database.error: USD Primitive '/ASSET/mesh' has no material assigned
troubleshooting_database.fix: In Solaris (LOPs), ensure a Material Assign node is targeting the primitive path '/ASSET/mesh'.
The render node is trying to reference a light that doesn't exist. Check the 'Force Lights' or 'Candidate Lights' parameters.
The material path is invalid or the material was deleted. Update the 'shop_materialpath' attribute or the material parameter on the OBJ node.
The renderer cannot find the image at diffuse.jpg. Check your $HIP and $JOB paths or ensure the file was exported.
Ensure the render engine (Karma, Mantra, Redshift) is installed and the license is active.

troubleshooting_database.error: Geometry attribute 'Cd' not exported to renderer
troubleshooting_database.fix: Check the ROP node settings to ensure the attribute 'Cd' is included in the 'Attributes to Export' list.

troubleshooting_database.error: Sampling error in material /mat/principled1: Out of memory
troubleshooting_database.fix: The shader is too complex or the texture resolution is too high for the available GPU/CPU memory. Optimize your textures or simplify the shader graph.

troubleshooting_database.error: USD Primitive '/ASSET/mesh' has no material assigned
troubleshooting_database.fix: In Solaris (LOPs), ensure a Material Assign node is targeting the primitive path '/ASSET/mesh'.
The render node is trying to reference a light that doesn't exist. Check the 'Force Lights' or 'Candidate Lights' parameters.
The material path is invalid or the material was deleted. Update the 'shop_materialpath' attribute or the material parameter on the OBJ node.
The renderer cannot find the image at diffuse.jpg. Check your $HIP and $JOB paths or ensure the file was exported.
Ensure the render engine (Karma, Mantra, Redshift) is installed and the license is active.
